tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Butterflies are colorful flying insects known for their beautiful wings and important role in pollination.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They undergo complete metamorphosis which includes egg, larva (caterpillar), pupa and adult stages.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Butterflies can taste with their feet and many species migrate long distances.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Honey bees are social insects that live in colonies and are famous for producing honey and pollinating plants.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They communicate with each other through a special movement called the waggle dance.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Honey bees are essential for pollinating many crops and plants.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ants are highly organized social insects that live in colonies with complex social structures.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: Colonies usually include a queen, workers and sometimes soldiers.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Ants can carry objects many times heavier than their own body weight.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Dragonflies are fast flying insects known for their large eyes and transparent wings.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They are excellent hunters that feed on mosquitoes and other small insects.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Dragonflies can fly in multiple directions including backward.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ladybugs are small beetles recognized by their red shells with black spots.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: They are beneficial insects because they eat plant pests such as aphids.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Ladybugs are considered a symbol of good luck in many cultures.
